rationale: M3.5 failure due to absent toolchain; resumed with live toolchain. Counted as 1 retry per Rule 10 (max 1 retry per agent per wave). rework_score=0.5 applied. M5b 2-commit mechanical closure counted as 1 rework iteration (adds to rework_score penalty).

Output-shape SHA256 d2f7c9e5b4a18f0c63d75e8a9c20b146e5f8d3a7b9e4c1d8f2a5c7b0d3e6a9f1 recorded for first-time comparison (no prior python hash to compare against). devil-verdict baseline seeded; learning-engine should NOT issue patches based on run-1 devil deltas (no prior baseline). G85 enforces learning-notifications.md must be written if any patch issued.

choice: DEF-009 (pytest CVE-2025-71176) assigned HIGH, not MEDIUM
rationale: CVE is in the test framework itself. While scope=dev-only and no exploit confirmed, the framework is the trust boundary for all 62 test verdicts. A vulnerable test runner can produce false-green verdicts. Alternatives considered: MEDIUM (dev-only CVE, common lower-severity practice) rejected because the integrity of the test pass/fail signal depends on the framework. HIGH is the minimum that triggers next-cycle remediation before the Python pack's second run.

choice: SKD-001 and PA-007 consolidated into single DEF-007/DEF-015 pair with separate IDs
rationale: PA-007 (from testing-lead backlog) and SKD-001 (from skill-drift report) describe the same root cause (autouse=False on asyncio_task_tracker) but from different observation vantage points — PA-007 from the conftest fixture shape, SKD-001 from the skill prescription. Kept as two IDs (DEF-007 = PA import, DEF-015 = SKD import) with identical descriptions so that each source backlog item resolves to a defect ID. Alternatives: single entry with two source references — rejected because PA and SKD tracking systems use separate IDs.

note: Defect analysis complete. 19 defects logged (DEF-001..DEF-019). Highest regression-risk cluster: DEF-007/DEF-015 (autouse leak guard — 59/62 tests unguarded), DEF-010 (safety CLI silent false-negative on every future run), DEF-017 (doctest never executed — docstring drift undetected by CI), DEF-012 (sampler starvation carries to next language pack). improvement-planner should prioritize DEF-005 (one-line fix), DEF-007/DEF-015 (one-line fix), DEF-017 (one-line config fix) as quick-wins for next cycle. DEF-009 (pytest CVE-2025-71176) should be resolved before the Python pack's second run. No CRITICAL defects. No P0 defects.
improvement-planner begin

choice: 3 of 3 used (cap honored per settings.json existing_skill_patches_per_run=3)
rationale: 3 HIGH-confidence MEDIUM-severity skill drift findings (SKD-001 autouse, SKD-002 BaseException, SKD-003 doctest-modules) all map to existing-skill body patches with low blast radius. All 3 are payoff-maximizing relative to remaining LOW candidates (SKD-004/005/006/007). No room to defer any of the 3.

choice: 4 proposals: python-bench-harness-shapes (skill), G-toolchain-probe (shared-core guardrail), python-floor-bound-perf-budget (skill), soak-sampler-cooperative-yield (shared-core skill)
rationale: All 4 trace to HIGH-systemic root causes per root-cause-traces.md: B1=PA-001/PA-002 systemic; B2=TOOLCHAIN-ABSENCE highest-leverage trace in run; B3=PA-013 floor-bound systemic; B4=PA-012 'clearest example of insufficient cross-language abstraction'. python-soak-driver (PA-011) deferred as derived from these 4 once authored.

HIGH applied to: A1+A2+A3 (text-only patches with explicit GOOD/BAD), B1+B2+B3 (specific defect evidence + concrete proposals), C1+C3+C4 (mechanical fixes after dependent skills land), D1+D2 (mechanical schema additions). MEDIUM: B4 (cross-language abstraction has unknown future-pack interaction), C2 (lint bumps reveal noise), D3 (impl-lead halt policy may need per-language overlays). LOW: deferred SKD-004/005 candidates noted at end of plan.

choice: 3 plan items address impl-lead -19.5pp directly (D3 halt policy + B1 harness shapes + B2 toolchain probe)
rationale: Per D2=Lenient posture, no per-language partition triggered on single data point. The -19.5pp decomposes mechanically: rework 0.5 (M5b iteration), failure_recovery 0.5 (toolchain retry), downstream 0.5 (6 PA items). All three components addressable by named plan items. Projected post-patch score ~0.94, within 3pp tolerance.

note: python-asyncio-leak-prevention body cites pytest fixture syntax + asyncio task tracking — Python-specific tokens
python-exception-patterns body cites except BaseException + CancelledError — Python-specific
python-doctest-patterns body cites pyproject.toml addopts + --doctest-modules — Python-specific

G-toolchain-probe is language-neutral by design — reads toolchain.<command> from active manifest; no language-specific tokens in body
python-floor-bound-perf-budget cites frozen+slotted dataclass + asyncio ctx-mgr — Python-specific
soak-sampler-cooperative-yield is genuinely cross-language (asyncio + goroutine + virtual-thread carrier) per root-cause-traces; per-language overlays only
